sql_array: CREATE TABLE array_test (id Int32, values Array(Int32), idx Int32, value Int32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, arrayConcat(values, [idx % value]) AS combined, (arrayConcat(values, [idx % value]) % length(values)) AS mod_result FROM array_test WHERE mod_result > 0;

SELECT id, arrayConcat((SELECT arrayConcat(values, [value]) FROM array_test WHERE idx = id), [idx % 2]) AS combined_result FROM array_test WHERE idx > 0 GROUP BY id HAVING length(combined_result) < 5;

SELECT id, arrayConcat(values, [value % 3]) AS combined_value FROM (SELECT id, values, (SELECT count(*) FROM array_test WHERE idx < id) AS non_matching FROM array_test) AS derived WHERE non_matching > 0 ORDER BY combined_value;
SELECT id, (arrayConcat(values, [idx % CAST(NULL AS Int32)]) % 2) AS mod_with_null FROM array_test;
SELECT id, arrayConcat(values, [value % 10]), (arrayConcat(values, [value % 10]) % max(value)) AS mod_max FROM array_test GROUP BY id HAVING mod_max = 0;
SELECT id, arrayConcat(values, [5.5 % value]) AS combined FROM array_test WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY combined LIMIT 10;
SELECT id, arrayConcat([values, arrayConcat([1, 2], [3])]) AS deeper_concat FROM array_test WHERE id = 4;
